revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23984597483|manual triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[ja:深志神社]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:Fukashi-jinja haiden.jpg|prefecture={{ill|Fukashi|qid=Q11560041|1=Fukashi}}|coordinates={{Coord|36.23|N|137.9738888888889|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|founded_by={{ill|Ogasawara Sadamune|qid=Q7079835|1=Ogasawara Sadamune}}|deity={{ill|Takeminakata|qid=Q9150391|1=Takeminakata}}; {{ill|Tenjin|qid=Q1753428|1=Tenjin (kami)}}}} {{infobox shinto shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | image = Fukashi-jinja haiden.jpg | name = Fukashi Shrine | location = Nagano Prefecture, Matsumoto City, Fukashi 3-7-43 | typeDeg = 36 | typeMin = 13 | typeSec = 48 | N (North Rank) or S (South Rank) = N | longitudeDeg = 137 | longitudeMin = 58 | longitudeSec = 26 | E (East longitude) or W (West longitude) = E | map Country Code = JP | deity = [[:en:Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|Takeminakata-no-Mikoto]]
{{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|en|Sugawara no Michizane|qid=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} | Foundation = 2nd year of Ryakuo (1339) | Shrine Rank = [[Prefectural Shrine]]
[[Betsubyo Shrine]] | festival = {{ill|July 24|en|July 24|qid=Q2721|lt=July 24|1=July 24}} and {{ill|July 25|en|July 25|qid=Q2720|lt=July 25|1=July 25}} | Rituals = City God festival | image =
Haiden | map = Nagano }} [[File:Fukashi-jinja haiden.jpg|260px|Fukashi Shrine]]{{ill|Fukashi Shrine|ja|%E6%B7%B1%E5%BF%97%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukashi Shrine|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}} is a [[Beppyo Shrine]] near the Matsumoto Museum of Art and the Performing Arts Centre{{Cite web |title=Fukashi Shrine {{!}} Spots To Visit |url=https://welcome-matsumoto.com/spots/spots-to-visit/fukashi-shrine/ |access-date=2023-10-20 |website=Welcome-Matsumoto |language=en-US}}. It is dedicated to the Shinto deities {{ill|Tenjin (kami)|en|Tenjin (kami)|qid=Q1753428|lt=Tenjin|1=Tenjin (kami)}} and [[Takeminakata|Suwamyojin]]. In summer, the Tenjin festival happens here. 18 wooden floats from nearby areas come to the shrine..{{Cite web |title=Fukashi Shrine |url=https://visitmatsumoto.com/en/spot/fukashi-shrine/ |access-date=2023-10-19 |website=Visit Matsumoto |language=en-US}} ==Overview== {{Right| }} '''Fukashi Shrine''' (Fukashi Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in [[Fukashi (Matsumoto)|Fukashi]], [[:en:Matsumoto City|Matsumoto City]], {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|qid=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture|1=Nagano Prefecture}}. It was formerly a [[Prefectural Shrine]] and is currently a [[Beppyo Shrine]] under the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. It is commonly known as "Tenjin-sama." The main deities enshrined are [[:en:Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|Takeminakata-no-Mikoto]] from [[:en:Suwa Grand Shrine|Suwa Grand Shrine]] and {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|en|Sugawara no Michizane|qid=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} (revered as {{ill|Tenjin|en|Tenjin|qid=Q1305727|lt=Tenjin|1=Tenjin}}). == History == [[File:Fukashi-jinja01n4592.jpg|thumb|none|220px|Torii]] According to shrine tradition, [[Sadamune Ogasawara]], the governor of Shinano Province, who frequently dreamt of Suwa Myojin (Takeminakata-no-Mikoto), founded the shrine in the 2nd year of [[:en:Ryakuo|Ryakuo]] (1339) in gratitude for his victory in battle. Initially, it was called Miyamura Shrine (Miyamura Daimyojin). In the 9th year of {{ill|Oei|en|Oei|qid=Q2007444|lt=Oei|1=Oei}} (1402), [[Nagamoto Ogasawara]] enshrined {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|en|Sugawara no Michizane|qid=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} from [[:en:Kitano Tenmangu|Kitano Tenmangu]].[[File:Fukashi-jinja04st3200.jpg|thumb|none|220px|Interior of Haiden]] In the 1st year of [[:en:Eisho|Eisho]] (1504), when the retainers of the {{ill|Ogasawara clan|en|Ogasawara clan|qid=Q2975340|lt=Ogasawara clan|1=Ogasawara clan}} built Fukashi Castle (later {{ill|Matsumoto Castle|en|Matsumoto Castle|qid=Q739612|lt=Matsumoto Castle|1=Matsumoto Castle}}), the shrine was revered as the guardian and tutelary deity of the castle, with the main hall facing west. In the 2nd year of {{ill|Tenbun|en|Tenbun|qid=Q1192853|lt=Tenbun|1=Tenbun}} (1533), after [[Nagataka Ogasawara]] was defeated by {{ill|Takeda Shingen|en|Takeda Shingen|qid=Q276404|lt=Takeda Shingen|1=Takeda Shingen}} and Shinano came under the control of the [[Takeda clan]], the shrine fell into disrepair. In the 10th year of {{ill|Tensho|en|Tensho|qid=Q7700654|lt=Tensho|1=Tensho}} (1582), the Ogasawara clan returned to Shinano, repaired the shrine buildings, and relocated the spirit of the Tenmangu (Kamata Tenjin) from Kamata Village, located south of Fukashi Castle, to the new shrine north of Miyamura Shrine. Although Takeminakata-no-Mikoto was originally the primary deity, Tenmangu became more famous, and the shrine came to be known as Fukashi Tenjin or Tenjin-sama. During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|qid=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, the shrine was venerated by successive lords of [[Matsumoto Domain]] and served as the general tutelary deity for the southern half of the merchant town of Matsumoto. In the 12th year of [[:en:Tenpo|Tenpo]] (1841), with permission from the [[Urabe clan|Yoshida family]] of Kyoto, the shrine's name was changed from Miyamura Shrine to the current name. In Showa 3, it was Ranked as a prefectural shrine. == Cultural Properties == *City-designated Tangible Folk Cultural Property The stage of Matsumoto Castle Town - A work from the late {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|qid=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}. == Access == *About a 15-minute walk from [[Matsumoto Station]] on {{ill|JR|en|JR|qid=Q127942|lt=JR|1=JR}} *About 15 minutes from [[Matsumoto Interchange]] on [[:en:Chuo Expressway|Chuo Expressway]] == References == == Bibliography == *Edited by [[Kenichi Tanigawa]], "Japanese Gods - Shrines and Sacred Sites - 9 Mino, Hida, Shinano", 2000, [[Hakusuisha]] == External Links == *[http://www.fukashi-tenjin.or.jp/ Fukashi Shrine] *[http://www.baifukaku.jp/ Fukashi Shrine Baifukaku] *[http://www.i-turn.jp/matsumoto-fukashi-jinzy.html Introduction Site] {{Commonscat|Fukashi-jinja}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Tenjin Faith}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] [[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]] {{translated page|ja|深志神社|version=102002855|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{everybodywiki link|Fukashi Shrine}} {{Commons category|Fukashi-jinja}} [[ja:深志神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11560040}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Matsumoto]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Matsumoto]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Matsumoto, Nagano]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Suwa shrines]] [[Category:Tenjin (kami)]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
